steps.title: Identify the subject and find a finite verb
steps.content: The subject is The extensor digitorum brevis — a single muscle. The phrase "which is attached to the calcaneus" is a relative clause; the main clause still needs a finite verb, not a participle or infinitive.

steps.title: Match a singular finite verb
steps.content:
bears (a singular present-tense verb) agrees with the singular subject and works as the main verb. Bearing and having borne are participles (not finite verbs), and to bear is an infinitive — none can serve as the main verb of the sentence.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
